commutative_reading.candidate_to_gate: Candidate models are preserved as a typed set; the gate morphism maps them to accept/reject decision artifacts.
commutative_reading.gate_to_claim: The accepted model alone is insufficient; the claim is composed from gate, stress-test, and regime-transition artifacts.
commutative_reading.rejected_paths: Rejected alternatives remain in the provenance category as explicit failed explanatory paths.

morphisms.description: Assemble accepted and rejected candidate models from computational mechanics evidence.
morphisms.name: instantiate_mechanics_candidate_models

morphisms.description: Preserve the gate-selected explanatory model as a typed accepted model artifact.
morphisms.name: accept_mechanics_model

morphisms.description: Preserve a failed alternative as a typed rejected model artifact rather than deleting it.
morphisms.name: reject_mechanics_model

morphisms.description: Map candidate models to accepted/rejected decisions using AIC, BIC, or an explicit mechanics criterion.
morphisms.name: apply_model_selection_gate

morphisms.description: Apply an ablation or perturbation showing what the accepted model explains beyond a simpler descriptor.
morphisms.name: run_mechanics_stress_test

morphisms.description: Record old regime, transported artifacts, richer regime, and residual content.
morphisms.name: audit_mechanics_regime_transition

morphisms.description: Compose gate, stress-test, and regime-transition artifacts into a mechanics claim.
morphisms.name: synthesize_categorical_mechanics_claim

objects.description: Finite set of competing mechanics model artifacts considered for a run.
objects.name: MechanicsCandidateModelSet

objects.description: Model artifact accepted by a deterministic mechanics gate.
objects.name: MechanicsAcceptedModel

objects.description: Model artifact rejected but preserved as residual provenance.
objects.name: MechanicsRejectedModel

objects.description: Typed gate artifact mapping candidate models to accept/reject decisions.
objects.name: MechanicsModelSelectionGate

objects.description: Ablation or perturbation artifact testing accepted-model informativeness.
objects.name: MechanicsStressTest

objects.description: Artifact recording transport from a simple descriptor regime to a richer mechanics regime.
objects.name: MechanicsRegimeTransition

objects.description: Mechanics-language claim derived from gate, stress test, and regime transition artifacts.
objects.name: MechanicsDiscoveryClaim

problem_scope.specific_question: For the deterministic 7x7 synthetic curvature field, does a Helfrich-style curvature-energy proxy add material mechanics beyond a curvature-only shape descriptor?
problem_scope.system_boundary: Synthetic computational membrane grid from -1.5 to 1.5 um in x/y, mean curvature in 1/um, bending modulus 20 kBT, tension metadata retained but not used in the quadratic proxy.

discovery_report.limitations: The curvature field and material model are deterministic synthetic_computational inputs, not measured membrane geometry or calibrated biophysical parameters.
mechanics_equations_or_formal_descriptor: Total grid energy: E = sum_i e_i.
Bending-modulus sensitivity: E(alpha kappa) = alpha E(kappa).

model_selection_gate.decision_rule: Accept the model that maps curvature and bending modulus to energy with units and sensitivity diagnostics.

problem_scope.specific_question: For the deterministic 7x7 synthetic curvature field, does a Helfrich-style curvature-energy proxy add material mechanics beyond a curvature-only shape descriptor?
problem_scope.system_boundary: Synthetic computational membrane grid from -1.5 to 1.5 um in x/y, mean curvature in 1/um, bending modulus 20 kBT, tension metadata retained but not used in the quadratic proxy.

regime_transition_audit.audit_claim: The richer regime preserves the old descriptor artifacts and adds explanatory mechanics content that was not present in the simple regime.

rejected_alternatives.name: topology-only or curvature-only membrane claim
rejected_alternatives.why_rejected: It cannot express bending-modulus sensitivity or energy localization.

discovery_report.scientific_claim: The membrane run supports a curvature-energy mechanics claim: the synthetic curvature field has localized bending-energy structure whose total magnitude is controlled by the assigned bending modulus.
discovery_report.scientific_hypothesis: A membrane curvature field becomes mechanically interpretable only when transported into a curvature-energy regime with bending modulus dependence.

stress_test_or_ablation.interpretation: Energy scales linearly with bending modulus, confirming that the accepted claim is an energy-regime claim rather than a geometry-only summary.
stress_test_or_ablation.name: bending-modulus sensitivity

discovery_report.title: Membrane curvature-energy mechanics

required_evidence: membrane geometry or curvature field with units
material parameters such as bending modulus or tension
computational boundary conditions or perturbation protocol

skill_routing.purpose: measure membrane geometry/curvature from microscopy outputs
load curvature, geometry, or perturbation tables
fit curvature-energy or transition trends with diagnostics
perform modal/field mechanics only if mesh and material JSON exist

investigator_principle: Act like a mechanics investigator, not a generic data analyzer.
mechanical_hypothesis: Membrane curvature and patch-gluing constraints can define energy and shape-transition mechanics.
mechanical_question: Can membrane geometry, curvature measurements, and material parameters support quantitative energy or shape-transition estimates?

quantitative_computational_mechanics_res.method_or_skill_used: ScienceClaw csv-read on generated computational curvature CSV plus deterministic Helfrich-style quadratic curvature-energy summary
quantitative_computational_mechanics_res.name: Synthetic membrane curvature-energy computation
quantitative_computational_mechanics_res.scientific_interpretation: The curvature field yields a compact bending-energy summary for the membrane model: the RMS curvature and total quadratic energy proxy quantify how far the synthetic patch departs from flat geometry under the assigned bending modulus. Mechanically, the result supports a curvature-energy interpretation rather than a topology-only membrane descriptor.
quantitative_computational_mechanics_res.uncertainty_or_limitation: Deterministic synthetic computational curvature field and material model; useful for energy-pipeline computation, not a measured membrane shape.
